questions.text: What is a token in the context of large language models?
options: A complete sentence or paragraph of text
The smallest unit a model processes, typically a word or subword
A hidden layer in the neural network
questions.correctAnswer: The smallest unit a model processes, typically a word or subword

questions.text: Which tokenizer approach breaks words into subword units?
What does a 4K context window mean?
options: The model can process up to 4,000 tokens at once
The model has 4 kilobytes of memory
The model was trained on 4 thousand documents
The model outputs are 4 kilobytes in size
questions.correctAnswer: The model can process up to 4,000 tokens at once

questions.text: Explain why different languages might have different token counts for the same concept (e.g., 'hello' vs 'hola'). What does this imply about model performance across languages?
Which model mentioned in Module 1 has the largest context window?
What is the purpose of special tokens like [CLS], [SEP], or <bos>?
options: To add padding to reach context length
To mark important sections and control model behavior
questions.correctAnswer: To mark important sections and control model behavior

questions.text: How would you estimate the token count for a typical English paragraph before feeding it to a language model? Provide a rough calculation method.
